title: What grit removes brush marks in latex paint?
problem: Dried latex paint can show brush ridges or uneven texture.
likely_cause: Brush marks must be dry enough to sand cleanly before they can be leveled.
recommended_grit: Use 320 or 400 to level brush marks, then 600 for smoother repaint prep.
steps: Let the paint dry enough to sand cleanly.
Refine with 600 for smoother visible areas.
avoid: Do not sand latex paint that still feels soft or gummy.
success_check: The brush ridges are level and the surface is ready for another coat.
quick_answer: For dried latex brush marks, use 320 or 400, then 600 before repainting.
mistakes_to_avoid: Do not use coarse grit on visible wall or trim paint.
Do not paint over sanding dust.
search_phrases: what grit removes brush marks in latex paint
latex paint brush ridges sanding grit

title: What grit fixes roller marks after painting?

avoid: Do not sand paint that still smears.
